pattern: Use KMP to find all occurrences of both patterns in the main string, then use two-pointer or binary search to pair each occurrence of 'a' with a nearby occurrence of 'b' within distance k.
hints: First collect all start positions where 'a' appears in s and all positions where 'b' appears, using KMP or a similar linear-time string search.
For each position i where 'a' starts, you need to check if any 'b' occurrence j satisfies |i - j| <= k; sort the 'b' positions and binary-search for the closest one.
Return every valid 'a' index in sorted order; since KMP scans left to right the list is already sorted.

edge_inputs.description: a and b are the same string, index 0 qualifies
no occurrence of a in s
a occurs but no b occurrence within k
single character s equals a and b
k equals s length, every a-occurrence qualifies if any b exists
b occurrence exists but just outside k distance on both sides
multiple a occurrences, only some within k of a b

stress.description: random lowercase string with short repeated a and b patterns
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = size
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(n))
    a = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5)))
    b = ''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5)))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, a, b, k]
